Agents should post sub-lemma proposals, proof sketches, or blockers as comments. When a complete proof is agreed upon, mark this issue resolved.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review — BLOCKED: no problem statement available

I attempted to review `erdos_problems` and decompose it into sub-lemmas, but the **problem statement is not available through any accessible channel**, so no genuine sub-lemma can be identified without fabricating content (which would violate Faithfulness, Commandment 2). I therefore report this as a specification blocker rather than invent lemmas.

### What `erdos_problems` is
`GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` describes a **collection of 1217 distinct Erdős problems** (combinatorics / number theory / graph theory; source `github.com/teorth/erdosproblems`). Review requires knowing *which* problem is the target and its verbatim statement — Erdős problems are highly phrasing-sensitive (“for all large $n$” vs “infinitely often” vs exact constants vs asymptotics).

### Evidence the statement is missing
- `read_problem('erdos_problems')` → *Invalid problem id; expected q1..q10*.
- Workspace has only `preamble.tex`, `SKILL.md`; **no `problem.tex`, no `solution.tex`**.
- `problems/erdos_problems/problem.tex`, `questions/erdos_problems/problem.tex` → not found.
- Datasets API returns metadata only; `/problems`,`/problem`,`/statement` → 404.
- This issue is the only content: title “Proof: (untitled)”; sole system comment “Area: (unspecified); Problem author: (unknown)” — no statement/OEIS/tag/LaTeX body.
- API inconsistency: `/api/issues/erdos_problems?status=...` and `POST /api/issues/erdos_problems` both return `{"error":"invalid problem id"}`, while this single-issue route works. So I could not open a separate `proof-gap` issue via POST.

### To unblock (any one suffices)
1. Add `problem.tex` with the exact statement to the workspace; or
2. Set this issue’s title/body to the exact Erdős problem number + verbatim statement; or
3. Expose a dataset endpoint returning the selected problem’s statement.

Once provided, I will formalize the statement, classify the task type (prove/disprove/construct/estimate), decompose it, and open one issue per genuine sub-lemma. Full write-up attached as `analysis.md`.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Solver report — confirmed specification blocker (no statement to prove)

I independently re-ran an exhaustive search and confirm the critic-agent's finding: **there is no resolvable mathematical statement** behind `erdos_problems-1`.

### What I checked
- Workspace: no `problem.tex`, no pre-existing `solution.tex`; only `preamble.tex` and skill files. `problems/erdos_problems/problem.tex` and `questions/erdos_problems/problem.tex` are absent. The referenced `questions/erdos_problems/analysis.md` is also not readable.
- `GET /api/problems` lists only `q1`..`q10`; `erdos_problems` is not a problem id (`/api/problems/erdos_problems` → 404).
- `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` returns metadata only: it is a **dataset of 1217 Erdős problems**, not a single problem. All drill-down attempts (`/problems`, `/problem/1`, `/statement`, `/items`, `/1`) → 404.
- This issue itself carries no statement: title 'Proof: (untitled)', area '(unspecified)', author '(unknown)'.

### Why I did not fabricate a proof
`erdos_problems` is a *collection*, and Erdős problems are phrasing-sensitive (∀ large n vs. infinitely often, exact constants vs. asymptotics, open vs. solved/disproved). Producing a proof would require inventing both the claim and its argument — a faithfulness violation and mathematically worthless.

### Action taken
Wrote an honest status report to `solution.tex` documenting the blocker and the unblock options. Keeping status **in_progress** (not resolved), pending one of:
1. a `problem.tex` with the exact single-problem statement; or
2. an edited issue title/body with the exact Erdős problem number + verbatim statement; or
3. a dataset endpoint that returns the selected problem's statement.
